message:
test(fuzz): add ChunkedObjectManifest reconstruct + hash-binding fuzz target (resolves 0ojvm)

ChunkedObjectManifest (chunk.rs:22-193) is the NORMATIVE chunked-object
manifest with BLAKE3 end-to-end verification. Existing fcp-raptorq fuzz
targets (raptorq_roundtrip, raptorq_decode_bounds, raptorq_envelope_decrypt)
do NOT touch the chunk-manifest surface.

A regression in reconstruct could:
- drop hash verification → attacker substitutes chunks, payload
  reconstructs to attacker-chosen bytes
- panic on chunk_size=0 from a deserialized peer manifest → remote DoS
  via division-by-zero in chunk_size_at last-chunk modular arithmetic
  (chunk.rs:170-172 explicitly guards this)

Eight properties:
- from_payload + reconstruct round-trip
- chunk_count agrees with raw chunks count
- payload_hash tamper → HashMismatch
- too-few/too-many chunks → MissingChunks
- total_len tamper → LengthMismatch
- chunk-content bit-flip → HashMismatch (assembled-payload hash auth)
- reconstruct_unchecked accepts legitimate, rejects on count/length
  but NOT on hash (the documented asymmetry)

Once-gated regression anchors:
- (a) chunk_size=0 manifest → InvalidChunkSize from chunk_size_at, not
  panic (anti-DoS guard at chunk.rs:170-172)
- (b) payload_hash bit-flip → HashMismatch
- (c) Chunk-order swap on distinct-byte chunks → HashMismatch (order
  binding via assembled-payload hash)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -634,5 +634,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_chunk_manifest"
+path = "fuzz_targets/chunk_manifest.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
